title: rubus × rariglandulosus
intro: Rubus × rariglandulosus is a hybrid plant species within the Rubus genus, often referred to as a black raspberry or a hybrid of Rubus idaeus and Rubus occidentalis. The leaves, roots, and fruits of Rubus × rariglandulosus have been traditionally used in herbal medicine, with reported medicinal properties including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant's health benefits are attributed to its high content of anthocyanins and ellagic acid, which may help to protect against cell damage, reduce inflammation, and improve cardiovascular health. Rubus × rariglandulosus can be prepared for medicinal use through various herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and jams, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
